text.title: Figma vs Adobe 2026 Platform Comparison
text.summary: Market position, 2026 strategy focus, and decision rules for platform selection.

list.item: Figma — core strength: collaboration and UI; AI focus: workflow automation; target: product teams; pricing: per-seat; integration: API-first
Adobe CC — core strength: visual creation and enterprise; AI focus: content generation (Firefly); target: creative professionals; pricing: Creative Cloud bundle; integration: suite-integrated
Figma: end-to-end product development platform, AI-driven design and prototyping, developer handoff optimization, design system management
Adobe: content creation powerhouse, Firefly across all CC apps, enterprise licensing, multimedia production

rule.statement: For product teams in 2026, default to Figma (design/collaboration/handoff) + Firefly Services API (asset generation) as a combined stack.
rule.rationale: The two platforms are complementary, not mutually exclusive. Figma REST API offers the richer agent-automation surface. Firefly provides commercial-safe image generation. Using both eliminates the need to choose.

rule.statement: Revisit the platform decision every 6 months — tie the review to Figma Config and Adobe MAX annual conferences.
rule.rationale: Both platforms ship major AI feature changes at their annual conferences. Any comparison older than 6 months may reference retired or renamed features. Quarterly refresh keeps the decision current.

rule.statement: Document the toolchain decision in the project's AGENTS.md so agents operating on the project know which APIs to target.
rule.rationale: Agents cannot discover which platform a team uses from code alone. An explicit entry in AGENTS.md prevents agents from targeting the wrong API (Figma vs. Adobe) when automating design tasks.

antipattern.description: Applying a product-team comparison to a creative agency workflow.
antipattern.reason: "Best tool" conclusions do not transfer across team types. A product team prioritizes collaboration and dev handoff; a creative agency prioritizes asset creation and multimedia output. Use team-type-specific weights.

antipattern.description: Including pricing data from training data in a recommendation.
antipattern.reason: Pricing structures change. Always fetch current pricing from official sources before including it in a recommendation — per-seat vs. bundle calculations depend on team size and current plan tiers.
